title: Lastrea buchananii
taxonomy: Lastrea buchananii belongs to the family Athyriaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the lady ferns or wood ferns. This classification places Lastrea buchananii within the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. The genus Lastrea itself is often associated with the family Dryopteridaceae, although some sources may still group it under the family Athyriaceae. At the kingdom level, Lastrea buchananii is classified as Plantae. The species is further classified at the rank of species and sometimes also at the rank of subspecies, depending on the specific taxonomic classification being used.
uses: Lastrea buchananii is a type of fern that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its medicinal uses include treating respiratory issues and skin conditions. intro: Lastrea buchananii, also known as Buchanania fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is prized for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to support cardiovascular health and improve immune function. The bioactive constituents of Buchanania fern include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lastrea buchananii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions in traditional medicine.
